data.title: Bitcoin corrections have historically led to future rallies, despite drops. From Jan 2012 to May 2021, significant price drops occurred, like a 42.9% decrease in early 2012. More details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: A Bitcoin-focused media outlet, TFTC, emphasized the historical pattern of major BTC corrections leading to subsequent rallies. Analysis of a Bitcoin correction chart revealed numerous significant price drops in the asset's history, from government bans to hack-induced crashes. Despite each crash appearing catastrophic, they ultimately paved the way for future growth. Data comparing the period from January 2012 to May 2021 showcased the yearly high and low prices. For instance, in early 2012, Bitcoin plummeted by 42.9% between Jan. 12 and Jan. 27. This trend persisted over the years, with each correction followed by a substantial rally. Visit Benzinga.com for the complete story.

data.title: Dark Horse CPAs hires Joey Kinney as Principal Accelerator in Albuquerque, NM; begins Fractional CFO & Accounting for SMBs.
data.description: In Cardiff by the Sea, California on August 8, 2025, Dark Horse CPAs, renowned for its services to small businesses and individuals in accounting and tax matters, introduced Joseph Kinney, CPA, as the newest addition to its Principal Accelerator Program. Joseph Kinney, also known as Joey, will be taking on the role of Principal Accelerator within the program aimed at driving transformation within the firm.

data.title: AgriBank in St. Paul reports strong Q2 2025 financial results with high profitability, credit quality, and liquidity.
data.description: AgriBank in St. Paul has reported impressive financial results for the second quarter of 2025, showcasing robust profitability, credit quality, and liquidity. The continued success reflects the strength of the AgriBank business model, leading to positive outcomes for the Association. The announcement highlights the firm's commitment to excellence in financial performance, underscoring its position as a top-performing entity in the industry.

data.title: Colossal launches voting for Youth Athlete of the Year, hosted by Russell Wilson, sponsored by 3BRAND, benefiting Why Not You Foundation & V Foundation for Cancer Research.
data.description: Phoenix, on August 8, 2025 - Colossal, a renowned fundraiser known for organizing dynamic cause-based competitions, has initiated the public voting process for Youth Athlete of the Year. This campaign, conducted on a nationwide scale, aims to honor young athletes who exemplify integrity and sportsmanship. The voting process allows the public to engage and select their choice for the most deserving youth athlete.

data.title: Home Genius Exteriors donates $25,000 to Ronald McDonald House Charities of Greater Washington, D.C. and assembles over 1,000 care items.
data.description: As part of the HGE Cares community impact initiative, employees of Home Genius Exteriors (HGE) have assembled more than 1,000 care items. This initiative underscores HGE's dedication to charitable efforts. In line with this commitment, HGE made a significant contribution today by donating $25,000 to the Ronald McDonald House. The donation reflects HGE's ongoing support for community causes and its mission to make a positive difference in Washington.

data.title: Terra Firma Energy Limited in London conducts strategic review in August 2025 to support UK's energy system evolution.
data.description: Terra Firma Energy Limited has recently released its Strategic Review Report for August 2025 in London. The report outlines the company's plans for building a cleaner tomorrow. Key numbers highlighted in the report include financial figures, environmental impact metrics, and growth projections. The company aims to emphasize sustainability and innovation in its operations moving forward.

data.title: Optimally partners with EOS® as an official licensee, providing workflow design and automation tools for business execution.
data.description: A company based in Kansas City, Kansas, known as Optimally, has recently become a licensee of EOS®. This partnership will allow Optimally to provide businesses with workflow design and automation tools aimed at assisting them in translating their strategic plans into actionable steps. This collaboration aims to enhance businesses' capabilities in executing strategies effectively.

data.title: NASA chooses Impulse Space for two OTV service studies, aiming at cost-efficient spacecraft delivery; aiding future missions.
data.description: A company called Impulse Space based in Redondo Beach, California has been chosen by NASA to conduct two orbital transfer vehicle (OTV) service studies. These studies will focus on finding more affordable methods to transport spacecraft to challenging space destinations. The selection was made by NASA's Launch Services Program through the agency's VADR (Venture-Class Ac). The studies aim to provide NASA with valuable information on the commercial potential of OTVs, assisting in future mission preparations.
